{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator",
  "description": "Bradfield Consulting Limited is a professional service firm created to provide human resource solutions for organizations of any size. Our goal is to help our esteemed clients eliminate issues bordering on having incompatible or inexperienced employees. Our partnership begins from attracting the right employees, settling them well at work and ensuring that they are equipped to deliver on your goals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator

Job Location:&nbsp;Wuse Zone 2, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job &nbsp;Description&nbsp;


	The Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator is responsible for managing, verifying, and coordinating documentation related to proof of funds requirements for clients, partners, or transactions.
	The role ensures that all submitted financial documents meet compliance standards, internal policies, and stakeholder expectations.
	The coordinator acts as a liaison between clients, financial institutions, and internal teams to facilitate smooth processing of funding confirmations.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate the collection and verification of Proof of Funds (POF) documentation from clients or partners.
	Review submitted financial documents to ensure completeness, authenticity, and compliance with internal requirements
	Liaise with banks, financial representatives, legal teams, and external stakeholders regarding funding confirmations.
	Track and monitor the status of POF submissions and approvals
	Maintain accurate and confidential records of financial documentation
	Support due diligence and compliance procedures related to financial verification
	Assist in preparing funding confirmation letters, reports, and supporting documentation.
	Ensure timelines for submission and verification processes are met
	Communicate updates to relevant internal teams and stakeholders
	Identify discrepancies or missing information and follow up accordingly
	Support transaction readiness by ensuring funding documentation is properly validated.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Accounting, or related field preferred
	Minimum 3 years&rsquo; experience in the same field in a bank
	Experience in marketing, documentation coordination, compliance support, banking coordination, or financial processing is an advantage
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Ability to handle confidential financial information with integrity
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook)
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience handling Proof of Funds letters
	Background in financial services, recruitment processing, escrow coordination, or transaction support environments
	Understanding of AML / KYC compliance procedures
	Strong coordination and stakeholder management skills


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail
	Confidentiality and integrity
	Communication skills
	Process coordination
	Documentation accuracy
	Time management
	Problem-solving ability.


Salary Range
N100,000 &ndash; N400,000 per month depending on experience

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;bradfieldforms@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator Job at Bradfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Bradfield Consulting Limited is a professional service firm created to provide human resource solutions for organizations of any size. Our goal is to help our esteemed clients eliminate issues bordering on having incompatible or inexperienced employees. Our partnership begins from attracting the right employees, settling them well at work and ensuring that they are equipped to deliver on your goals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator

Job Location: Wuse Zone 2, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job  Description 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Proof of Funds (POF) Coordinator is responsible for managing, verifying, and coordinating documentation related to proof of funds requirements for clients, partners, or transactions.
	The role ensures that all submitted financial documents meet compliance standards, internal policies, and stakeholder expectations.
	The coordinator acts as a liaison between clients, financial institutions, and internal teams to facilitate smooth processing of funding confirmations.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate the collection and verification of Proof of Funds (POF) documentation from clients or partners.
	Review submitted financial documents to ensure completeness, authenticity, and compliance with internal requirements
	Liaise with banks, financial representatives, legal teams, and external stakeholders regarding funding confirmations.
	Track and monitor the status of POF submissions and approvals
	Maintain accurate and confidential records of financial documentation
	Support due diligence and compliance procedures related to financial verification
	Assist in preparing funding confirmation letters, reports, and supporting documentation.
	Ensure timelines for submission and verification processes are met
	Communicate updates to relevant internal teams and stakeholders
	Identify discrepancies or missing information and follow up accordingly
	Support transaction readiness by ensuring funding documentation is properly validated.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Accounting, or related field preferred
	Minimum 3 years’ experience in the same field in a bank
	Experience in marketing, documentation coordination, compliance support, banking coordination, or financial processing is an advantage
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Ability to handle confidential financial information with integrity
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook)
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience handling Proof of Funds letters
	Background in financial services, recruitment processing, escrow coordination, or transaction support environments
	Understanding of AML / KYC compliance procedures
	Strong coordination and stakeholder management skills


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail
	Confidentiality and integrity
	Communication skills
	Process coordination
	Documentation accuracy
	Time management
	Problem-solving ability.


Salary Range
N100,000 – N400,000 per month depending on experience

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Group Head, Human Resources",
  "description": "Bradfield Consulting Limited is a professional service firm created to provide human resource solutions for organizations of any size. Our goal is to help our esteemed clients eliminate issues bordering on having incompatible or inexperienced employees. Our partnership begins from attracting the right employees, settling them well at work and ensuring that they are equipped to deliver on your goals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Group Head, Human Resources

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	The Human Resources Manager oversees all aspects of human resources (HR), its practices, and operations to meet the needs of the constantly evolving business.
	The role of HR Manager, is to localize and implement the Group&rsquo;s current people programs and service delivery strategies, enhancing organizational decision-making and approaches for securing growth on a business-wide scale.
	The candidate is an active member of the Medical Directors&rsquo; Committee as well as a key executive partner and coach who is tasked with implementing strategic planning and initiatives, change management, and organizational design.


Leadership/Supervisory Role:


	The Human Resources Manager (HRM) working with the Group HR implements long-range strategic planning to anticipate the innovation of change initiatives that promote people productivity, organizational efficiency, and cost effectiveness.
	Accountable and provides overall leadership and guidance to HR functions by overseeing, HR budgeting, talent acquisition, employee Relations/ engagement, training and development, career development, performance management, establishing structures, HR legal framework, responsible for culture, values etc, across Lagoon Hospitals
	The HRM is responsible for ensuring that the business&rsquo;s values are clearly communicated and understood at all levels of the business, clarifying on employee conduct that is necessary for realizing an engaging high performance culture within the business.
	Additionally, he/she ensures that the organizational design and rewards structure is reflective of a dynamic business environment and up-to-date modes of working in an increasingly competitive business environment.
	Where necessary, the HRM also intervenes and mediates conflicts in a manner that is reflective of the business and that leads to appropriate resolution.


Strategy:


	The HRM formulates, in conjunction with the Group Head HR, strategies that lead to quality and depth in the talent employed in the business and aligning workforce planning to business needs, growth and expansion while also developing the required skills needed to deliver the mission critical result.
	Developing and implementing comprehensive compensation and benefit strategies that are cost effective for the business, acts as the strategic business advisor to the Medical Directors and departmental heads about the workforce planning, engagement and associated costs.
	The HRM additionally functions as the strategic business advisor to the MDs and departmental heads in regard to key business and management issues.


Analytics:


	Collects, analyses and uses data for decision making. monitors, analyses, and identifies strategic needs, gaps, and risks using the data.
	In the analytical role, the HRM leverages on the ERP tools that identify, align, and build the business&rsquo;s human capital capabilities to achieve overall business results and reach desired targets.
	The HRM further uses findings of analyses in resolving issues and driving the business&rsquo;s agenda. HR


Budgeting:


	Conscientiously manage the staff cost, profitably drive each hospital facility staff cost as a percentage of revenue and in alignment with the staff cost budget.


Collaboration:


	This role is also highly collaborative and will collaborate with MDs and Group Head HR to drive the human capital agenda as a business partner to understand, analyse, and support the needs for the business, leading to the subsequent establishment of suitable strategies in alignment with the business agenda.
	The HRM also performs other operational duties as he/she deems fit for the proper execution of his/her duties or duties as assigned.


Learning &amp; Development:


	The HRM has the responsibility of developing and improving the Knowledge, Skill and Attitude (KSA) of the workforce to remain successful.
	Develop and implement learning strategies and programs. Build the business management and leadership skills for a strong management team.
	Design e learning courses, career plans, workshops and more while also maintaining all learning activities under budgets


Knowledge:


	The HRM keeps up with the latest industry trends and best practices, ensuring optimal performance of the HR department, which in turn influences the performance of the various departments and the business at large.


Compliance with regulators:


	Ensuring that HR process and policies are in alignment with the hospital accreditation regulatory body i.e. Joint Commission International (JCI)
	Immigration process for international staff
	&bull;Employee relation/ engagement- out of work bonding activities,
	&bull;Employee survey, compensation survey, coordination of townhall meeting,
	A team lead on HR Quality measurable elements, etc.
	Watch the labor space to ensure compliance with regulatory framework


Requirements
Education:


	First degree in humanities or social sciences and a master&rsquo;s degree in human resources related discipline.
	Registered member of the chattered Institute of personnel management of Nigeria (CIPMN), chattered Institute of personnel development (CIPD) and other related professional membership certifications


Experience:


	A candidate for this position will have had at least 10 years of working experience in a senior Human Resources position, preferably as a Head of HR &amp; Administration, HR Business Partner, Manager Training and Development, or Talent Acquisition Manager.
	The candidate will also have had proven and successful experience delivering innovative HR programs within a fast paced, evolving, and wide organizational setting.
	The candidate must also have had success managing a human resources team through a program and all the way to its completion and attainment of objectives.
	He/she will also have a demonstrated ability and experience advising andinfluencing business leaders, providing insights that lead to key decision makingwithin the business.
	A suitable candidate will also have had experience in policyformulating, development, and implementation of key HR strategies andprocedures.
	The candidate will additionally demonstrate strong businessacumen and general management experience and capabilities that enable himto effectively assess the internal business environment and delivertransformative change as necessary.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;bradfieldforms@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Group Head, Human Resources Job at Bradfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Bradfield Consulting Limited is a professional service firm created to provide human resource solutions for organizations of any size. Our goal is to help our esteemed clients eliminate issues bordering on having incompatible or inexperienced employees. We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Group Head, Human Resources

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities

	The Human Resources Manager oversees all aspects of human resources (HR), its practices, and operations to meet the needs of the constantly evolving business.

	Conscientiously manage the staff cost, profitably drive each hospital facility staff cost as a percentage of revenue and in alignment with the staff cost budget.

	This role is also highly collaborative and will collaborate with MDs and Group Head HR to drive the human capital agenda as a business partner to understand, analyse, and support the needs for the business, leading to the subsequent establishment of suitable strategies in alignment with the business agenda.
	The HRM also performs other operational duties as he/she deems fit for the proper execution of his/her duties or duties as assigned.


Learning & Development:


	The HRM has the responsibility of developing and improving the Knowledge, Skill and Attitude (KSA) of the workforce to remain successful.
	Develop and implement learning strategies and programs. Build the business management and leadership skills for a strong management team.
	Design e learning courses, career plans, workshops and more while also maintaining all learning activities under budgets


Knowledge:


	The HRM keeps up with the latest industry trends and best practices, ensuring optimal performance of the HR department, which in turn influences the performance of the various departments and the business at large.


Compliance with regulators:


	Ensuring that HR process and policies are in alignment with the hospital accreditation regulatory body i.e. Joint Commission International (JCI)
	Immigration process for international staff
	•Employee relation/ engagement- out of work bonding activities,
	•Employee survey, compensation survey, coordination of townhall meeting,
	A team lead on HR Quality measurable elements, etc.
	Watch the labor space to ensure compliance with regulatory framework


Requirements
Education:


	First degree in humanities or social sciences and a master’s degree in human resources related discipline.
	Registered member of the chattered Institute of personnel management of Nigeria (CIPMN), chattered Institute of personnel development (CIPD) and other related professional membership certifications


Experience:


	A candidate for this position will have had at least 10 years of working experience in a senior Human Resources position, preferably as a Head of HR & Administration, HR Business Partner, Manager Training and Development, or Talent Acquisition Manager.
	The candidate will also have had proven and successful experience delivering innovative HR programs within a fast paced, evolving, and wide organizational setting.
	The candidate must also have had success managing a human resources team through a program and all the way to its completion and attainment of objectives.
	He/she will also have a demonstrated ability and experience advising andinfluencing business leaders, providing insights that lead to key decision makingwithin the business.
	A suitable candidate will also have had experience in policyformulating, development, and implementation of key HR strategies andprocedures.
	The candidate will additionally demonstrate strong businessacumen and general management experience and capabilities that enable himto effectively assess the internal business environment and delivertransformative change as necessary.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control / Quality Assurance (QA/QC) Manager",
  "description": "AVGRET Consulting is dedicated to delivering excellence in construction projects and fostering innovation across diverse sectors. With a commitment to quality, safety, and sustainability, the organization aims to meet the highest industry standards while building long-term client relationships. Backed by a strong team of professionals and a robust portfolio, the company consistently ensures successful project execution and customer satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Quality Control / Quality Assurance (QA/QC) Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Role Description


	This is a full-time, on-site role for a QAQC (Quality Assurance and Quality Control) Manager in Lagos.
	The QAQC Manager will oversee quality management processes across construction projects, ensure compliance with relevant standards, and lead quality inspections.
	Responsibilities include developing quality control plans, supervising project teams to ensure adherence to quality protocols, conducting audits, and providing technical support to ensure high-quality project delivery. Collaboration with stakeholders and identifying opportunities for process improvement are integral to this role.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related fields
	Strong understanding of quality assurance processes, quality control procedures, and industry standards within construction.
	Ability to develop and implement quality control plans, inspection and testing protocols, and process improvement strategies.
	Proficiency in project management, team supervision, and stakeholder communication.
	Knowledge of construction safety regulations and relevant compliance requirements.
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Attention to detail and ability to conduct thorough quality assurance audits.
	Prior experience in a similar QAQC role within the construction industry preferred.
	Relevant certifications such as ISO 9001 or other quality management certifications are considered advantageous.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;(resume) to:&nbsp;savgretconsulting@gmail.com&nbsp;/&nbsp;hrbp2@rhemaprojectng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Quality Control / Quality Assurance (QA/QC) Manager Job at Savgret Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        AVGRET Consulting is dedicated to delivering excellence in construction projects and fostering innovation across diverse sectors. With a commitment to quality, safety, and sustainability, the organization aims to meet the highest industry standards while building long-term client relationships. Backed by a strong team of professionals and a robust portfolio, the company consistently ensures successful project execution and customer satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control / Quality Assurance (QA/QC) Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a full-time, on-site role for a QAQC (Quality Assurance and Quality Control) Manager in Lagos.
	The QAQC Manager will oversee quality management processes across construction projects, ensure compliance with relevant standards, and lead quality inspections.
	Responsibilities include developing quality control plans, supervising project teams to ensure adherence to quality protocols, conducting audits, and providing technical support to ensure high-quality project delivery. Collaboration with stakeholders and identifying opportunities for process improvement are integral to this role.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related fields
	Strong understanding of quality assurance processes, quality control procedures, and industry standards within construction.
	Ability to develop and implement quality control plans, inspection and testing protocols, and process improvement strategies.
	Proficiency in project management, team supervision, and stakeholder communication.
	Knowledge of construction safety regulations and relevant compliance requirements.
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Attention to detail and ability to conduct thorough quality assurance audits.
	Prior experience in a similar QAQC role within the construction industry preferred.
	Relevant certifications such as ISO 9001 or other quality management certifications are considered advantageous.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (resume) to: [email protected] / [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Accountant",
  "description": "Smart Nuel Communications Limited is a growing mobile technology retail company headquartered in Ikeja Computer Village, Lagos, one of Nigeria&rsquo;s largest technology markets. The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Junior Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Confirmation of customers bank receipts.
	Posting of all bank transactions (Receipts and Payments) .
	Stock Transfers Journal entries.
	Online bank payments.
	Issuance of cheques for outflows/payments.
	Cash flow management for Local purchase and order processing.
	Routine debtor circularization/verification.
	Routine stock count/verification.
	Timely postings, updating and reconciliations of supplier accounts. (Imports and local purchase)
	Routine cash count and verification.
	VAT and related payments processing.
	Monthly Reconciliations &amp; Preparation of Comprehensive Monthly reports.
	Liaise with Consultant on Tax and Accounting related issues.
	Supervisory role over the cashier and sales personnel to ensure correct postings.
	Assist the Store Manager with order processing.
	Manages relationships with all account officers.
	Monthly salaries computation and payments via bank advice or the bank portal.
	Sales target and Budget monitoring.
	Assist the HQ Branch Teamleads on office and staff administration matters.
	Maintain the fixed asset register.
	Any other related duty may be assigned from time to time by the Chairman or consultant.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor`s Degree with 2 - 3 years of experience.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to:&nbsp;jobs.smartnuel@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Junior Accountant Job at Smart Nuel Communications Limited

The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Accountant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities

	Confirmation of customers bank receipts.
	Posting of all bank transactions (Receipts and Payments) .
	Stock Transfers Journal entries.
	Online bank payments.
	Issuance of cheques for outflows/payments.
	Cash flow management for Local purchase and order processing.
	Routine debtor circularization/verification.
	Routine stock count/verification.
	Timely postings, updating and reconciliations of supplier accounts. (Imports and local purchase)
	Routine cash count and verification.

	Prepare Monthly Fixed Deposit Report of all excess funds-invest all excess funds of the company
	Prepare Weekly Book Balance Report
	Remit of all statutory Obligations before due dates

	Advanced General Ledger review and control
	Bank reconciliation preparation and review

Accountant Job at Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: CMS, Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Transactions posting into General Ledger (Lington & Bernie and Gadgas Limited
	Prepare Monthly Fixed Deposit Report of all excess funds-invest all excess funds of the company
	Prepare Weekly Book Balance Report
	Remit of all statutory Obligations before due dates
	Liaise with the Bank Accounts Officers, Tax Authorities, and External Auditors
	Liaise with tax officials for statutory bills issues.
	Prepare Vendor Payment Vouchers and Journal Vouchers for accruals


Requirement


	4 -5 years of experience in financial accounting principles (IFRS/GAAP)
	Advanced General Ledger review and control
	Bank reconciliation preparation and review


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
